rows.claim: A 'chip' UI component pattern already exists in the dashboard codebase and can be reused for the live status chip
rows.source_title: TopBar.tsx – existing chip usage in control panel

rows.claim: A 'chip' UI component pattern already exists in the dashboard codebase and can be reused for the live status chip
rows.source_title: HealthGrid.tsx – existing chip usage in health/status grid

rows.claim: The overview page already renders chip-style elements, confirming a reusable pattern for the Mission Control panel
rows.source_title: overview/page.tsx – chip rendering in main dashboard

rows.claim: Live pipeline status data is already being tracked server-side and can feed the chip
rows.source_title: cron_triggers.py – live and status references in pipeline scheduling

rows.claim: A cron store exists that tracks pipeline state, providing a backend source for live status
rows.source_title: cron_store.py – live state storage for pipelines

rows.claim: Observability tests for Mission Control already exist, reducing risk of undetected regressions
rows.source_title: test_mission_control_observability.py – live status observability tests

rows.claim: Linear pulse integration tracks status changes and could serve as a live data source for the chip
rows.source_title: linear_pulse.py – status tracking via Linear integration

rows.claim: The static frontend already references 'spec' and 'status', suggesting some pipeline status surface exists in the UI
rows.source_title: index.html – spec and status references in static frontend

rows.claim: No evidence of auth-gating or role checks on the Mission Control panel chip endpoint; boundary risks are unverified
rows.source_title: cron_triggers.py – status and live data exposed without confirmed auth layer

rows.claim: Live status polling from the chip could expose internal pipeline state to unauthorized dashboard viewers
rows.source_title: HealthGrid.tsx – chip renders status data with unknown auth boundary

rows.claim: No secrets or credentials are referenced in the chip or status components reviewed
rows.source_title: TopBar.tsx – chip component with no visible secret handling

rows.claim: Session recovery tests exist, suggesting auth session handling is tested but not confirmed for the new chip endpoint
rows.source_title: test_session_recovery.py – session and status recovery coverage

rows.claim: The proposal may be over-scoped if 'live' status requires real-time WebSocket or polling infrastructure not yet in place
rows.source_title: cron_triggers.py – cron-based triggers may not support true real-time live status

rows.claim: Chip pattern is already used in multiple dashboard components, so adding another is low-risk and not over-scoped
rows.source_title: overview/page.tsx – precedent for chip reuse reduces scope risk

rows.claim: Spec pipeline tests exist but do not confirm end-to-end live status propagation to the UI chip
rows.source_title: test_sdk_phase2.py – spec pipeline tests without confirmed UI status propagation

rows.claim: Specialised skill foundation tests reference 'spec' but do not validate Mission Control chip behavior
rows.source_title: test_specialised_skill_foundation.py – spec tests not covering chip UI

rows.claim: The smallest reversible version is a static chip reading from an existing cron_store status field, with no new backend required
rows.source_title: cron_store.py – existing status field usable for minimal chip implementation

rows.claim: Existing HealthGrid chip pattern can be cloned and feature-flagged for a safe, reversible rollout
rows.source_title: HealthGrid.tsx – chip component suitable for feature-flagged clone

rows.claim: Mission Control observability tests provide a safety net for reverting the chip if it causes regressions
rows.source_title: test_mission_control_observability.py – regression coverage for safe rollback

rows.claim: Next.js config and ESLint config are in place, so the new chip component will be linted and built consistently
rows.source_title: next.config.ts – build pipeline supports new dashboard components
